examples.title: Algorithm: Reverse Linked List
examples.description: Reverses a linked list in-place without creating new nodes
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReverseList
INPUT: head (reference to the head of the list)
OUTPUT: None (modifies the list in-place)

BEGIN
 prev = NULL
 current = head
 next = NULL

 WHILE current != NULL DO
 next = current->next
 current->next = prev
 prev = current
 current = next
 END WHILE

 head = prev
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode describes the iterative approach to reversing a linked list. It initializes three pointers (prev, current, and next) and iterates through the list, reversing the direction of the next pointers.

examples.title: Python: Reverse Linked List
examples.description: Reverses a linked list in-place without creating new nodes
examples.code:
class Node:
 def __init__(self, data):
 self.data = data
 self.next = None

class LinkedList:
 def __init__(self):
 self.head = None

 def reverse_list(self):
 prev = None
 current = self.head
 next_node = None

 while current:
 next_node = current.next
 current.next = prev
 prev = current
 current = next_node

 self.head = prev

# Example usage:
llist = LinkedList()
llist.head = Node(1)
llist.head.next = Node(2)
llist.head.next.next = Node(3)

llist.reverse_list()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation defines a Node class and a LinkedList class. The reverse_list method reverses the linked list in-place using the iterative approach.

examples.title: C: Reverse Linked List
examples.description: Reverses a linked list in-place without creating new nodes
examples.explanation: This C implementation defines a Node struct and a reverse_list function. The function reverses the linked list in-place using the iterative approach.
